text: Here's the video.
We made a posterior lateral thoracotomy and went to the fifth intercostal space.
And to prepare for possible interlobal bleeding, we opened the pericardium.
We came around the main PA.
and the upper PV, lower PV.
Looking at the fissure, the interlobar pleura was quite thickened due to prior chemoradiation, so we consider X-Site-2 dissection would be safer in order to spare the upper, left upper lobe.
So we decided on the excite to dissection and we asked the back table team to come down urgently.
We hyperionized with 3,000 of units and clamped the main PA.
and clamp the upper PV.
We divided the main PA at the level we would like to do anastomosis.
And also we divided the upper PV at the level we want to do anastomosis.
Regarding the bronchus, we divided the left main bronchus under the guidance of intraoperative bronchoscopy.
And we take the whole lung out.
And we started excitodissection.
First we separated the interlobar PA from the bronchus, and we divided the PA with scissors.
Because the tumor was invading the left upper lobe bronchus, we tried to divide the left upper lobe bronchus as peripherally as possible.
After we separated the left lower lobe, we had the back table team perfuse the upper lobe on the basis of our living donor transplant protocol.
After the perfusion, we brought it back on the tray
And we close the PA stump first.
And regarding the bronchus, we are required to do a bronchoplasty to connect the lingular bronchus and superior division bronchus.
And we put it back into the chest.
And we did an auto-transplant of the upper lobe, almost in the same way as a living donor lobe transplant.
And we placed a pericardial fat pad around the bronchial anastomosis
At the end of the surgery, the upper lobe showed a good aeration.
